title: trachylobium floribundum
intro: Trachylobium floribundum, also known as the "Gamboge tree," is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and small, greenish-yellow fruits. The plant's inner bark and sap contain a resinous substance called gamboge, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Gamboge has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and infusions, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
